error.signature: NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object

verdict.summary: Accessing a null component, GameObject, or unassigned Inspector reference. Most common Unity error.

dead_ends.action: Add null checks around every GetComponent call
dead_ends.why_fails: Excessive null checks hide design problems. Fix the root cause: assign references properly.

dead_ends.action: Use GameObject.Find() in Update() to always get fresh references
dead_ends.why_fails: Find() is O(n) over all GameObjects. Called every frame, it destroys performance.

workarounds.action: Assign references via Inspector (drag-and-drop) instead of runtime lookup
workarounds.how: [SerializeField] private Rigidbody rb;  // assign in Inspector, checked at edit time

workarounds.action: Use TryGetComponent to safely get components
Check if object was destroyed with the null-conditional operator
workarounds.how: myObject?.DoSomething();  // Unity overrides == null for destroyed objects
